records.title: Understand the Dutch civic-integration process
records.description: People required to integrate receive DUO and municipal instructions that identify the applicable law, start date, period, learning route and personal integration and participation plan.

official_source.title: Create a personal integration step-by-step plan

records.ai_summary: Treat the DUO letters, Mijn Inburgering and municipal PIP as authoritative for the personal route. Do not copy another person's exam list or deadline because different laws and circumstances apply.

records.title: Follow the PIP and assigned learning route
records.description: Under the current system the municipality conducts a broad intake and records a B1, education or self-reliance route and required activities in the personal integration and participation plan.
records.ai_summary: Ask the municipality to explain every PIP component, language level and participation activity before signing. Report changes or barriers early and keep the final plan with DUO correspondence.

records.title: Choose an accredited integration school
records.description: Some participants choose their own course provider while others receive a municipal choice, and DUO-linked financing can depend on using a school in the recognised register.
records.ai_summary: Verify accreditation in the official school register, compare lesson hours, group level, total price and cancellation terms, and ensure the course matches the PIP or applicable exam level.

records.title: Identify the integration exams required personally
records.description: Required language and knowledge exams depend on the civic-integration law, start date and learning route, with Mijn Inburgering and the PIP listing the applicable components.
records.ai_summary: Create an exam checklist from Mijn Inburgering rather than a generic list. Match each registration to the required language level and keep confirmations, payment evidence and exam rules.

records.title: Prepare for the integration exam location
records.description: DUO publishes the current locations for A2 language and knowledge exams, while B1 and B2 state exams use their own official location information.
records.ai_summary: Verify the location in the DUO invitation and official location list, plan enough travel time and bring the required identification. Check the separate Nt2 route for B1 or B2 exams.

records.title: Complete the required knowledge and participation components
records.description: Civic integration can include Knowledge of Dutch Society and, depending on the applicable law and route, municipal participation or labour-market components such as PVT, MAP or ONA.
records.ai_summary: Check which knowledge components appear in the PIP and Mijn Inburgering and whether the municipality or DUO records completion. Do not register for an obsolete component based on another person's route.

records.title: Check who pays for integration courses and exams
records.description: Integration costs depend on the applicable act and residence situation, with municipalities, personal payment or a DUO loan covering different courses and attempts under defined conditions.
records.ai_summary: Confirm the applicable act and funding route before signing a course contract or borrowing. Check whether the chosen school and exam attempt qualify, and retain invoices and DUO decisions.

records.title: Check integration exam results
records.description: DUO reports A2 language and knowledge exam results by letter and in Mijn Inburgering, while Nt2 B1 and B2 results follow the state-exam process.
records.ai_summary: Monitor Mijn Inburgering and official post after the stated processing period and verify that every passed component is recorded. Keep result letters until the diploma or certificate is available.

records.title: Retrieve an integration diploma or route certificate
records.description: After completing all required components, eligible participants can obtain a digital integration diploma through the official portal, while the self-reliance route ends with a municipal certificate.
records.ai_summary: Download and preserve the digital diploma through the secure portal and use it digitally as instructed. For a self-reliance-route certificate or missing record, contact the responsible municipality or DUO.

records.title: Respond to an integration deadline or fine notice
records.description: DUO may impose consequences when a required participant does not finish within the personal integration period and no valid extension or other relief applies.
records.ai_summary: Check the personal end date frequently and contact the municipality or DUO as soon as illness, waiting time or another obstacle threatens progress. Respond to preliminary notices with supporting evidence by the deadline.
